current (do not edit): Badger Supports is a peer-led mental health organization for UW–Madison students, offering support groups, workshops, and community events. Its communications make that support easy to find and act on.
The logo has two locked parts: the mark (the hand with the heart and lines) and the wordmark. They are always used together and scaled as one unit.
The palette is a primary red, a cream ground, charcoal text, and a set of program colors. Each program and topic is assigned a color so material is identifiable at a glance.
The type system uses two typefaces: More Sugar for headlines and Montserrat for all other text. Avoid a third typeface.
Layout follows three principles in every format: lead with the essential information, establish a clear visual order, and allow adequate spacing.
Badger Supports is photo-first: real student photos lead most pieces, with @irasutoya illustrations and the Barry mascot in support. Keep one style per piece.
The voice is warm, plain, and peer-to-peer. Messages lead with the reader's benefit and stay brief.
Material is designed to be readable and usable by everyone. Accessibility also broadens the audience a message reaches.
Badger Supports frequently partners with other organizations, including University Health Services, the Tenant Resource Center, and EBNS.
